thresholdSupport.notes: Wisconsin River Trips publishes route-specific Bluffton-gauge guidance for this Cattle Creek-to-Bluffton route: 305 cfs was more than plenty, 200 cfs is low, 150 cfs is scrape-prone, and roughly 700 cfs and up is likely too pushy. The app uses 200-500 cfs as the broad preferred window, 150 cfs as too low/scrapey, and 700+ cfs as beyond broad recreational recommendation.

candidates.route: Lenon Mill Park Access to Middle Raccoon River Access
candidates.blocker: Official Iowa DNR, Guthrie County, and Dallas County materials support the Middle Raccoon water-trail corridor from Lenon Mill Park Access through P-28 Access / Middle Raccoon River Access, and USGS 05483600 Middle Raccoon River at Panora is a direct live gauge in the route corridor. However, no official or strong route-specific published numeric paddling range was found. Rivers.MOHERP has useful Panora-gauge condition ratings and trip logs, and Iowa Parklands quotes Nate Hoogeveen's Paddling Iowa book that 400 cfs is a pleasant Middle Raccoon ride while describing a 600 cfs P28-to-Cowles outing as ideal, but those sources still do not provide an official or strong Lenon/P-28 route ladder suitable for implementation.
candidates.retryCondition: Retry only if Iowa DNR, Guthrie County, Dallas County, a local water-trail manager, Paddling Iowa-derived public source, Miles Paddled, Wisconsin River Trips, American Whitewater, or another credible paddling source publishes numeric Middle Raccoon route guidance tied to USGS 05483600 for Lenon Mill / P-28 / Middle Raccoon River Access trips.

thresholdSupport.notes: MilesPaddled records exact upper-Sugar trips at 53, 56, and 64 cfs on the Verona gauge, treating 53 cfs as just enough and warning that 2.5-3 ft is shallow while above 4 ft is wild and unruly. Wisconsin River Trips records 64 cfs as a good depth, treats about 100 cfs as a pretty full river, and cautions against high-water paddling because of fast current and downed trees. The app uses this as a guarded two-sided CFS model: 55-100 cfs preferred, below 50 cfs shallow/slow, and 200+ cfs outside casual recommendation.

candidates.route: Mines of Spain canoe launch round trip
candidates.blocker: Catfish Creek has a named public carry-down access at Mines of Spain and strong route-report evidence, but the level story depends on Mississippi River at Clayton stage rather than a direct live Catfish Creek gauge. Wisconsin River Trips explicitly notes there is no Catfish Creek gauge and that Mississippi backwater level is what matters near the mouth. That is useful local planning context, but it does not meet the Iowa route quality bar for a direct same-river/route-corridor gauge.
candidates.retryCondition: Retry only if USGS, Iowa DNR, Mines of Spain, Dubuque County, Wisconsin River Trips, Miles Paddled, or another credible source publishes Catfish Creek-specific live level data or a manager-endorsed route condition source strong enough to replace the Mississippi backwater proxy.

gauge.notes: USGS 05411500 Mississippi River at Clayton, IA is a live Mississippi River stage gauge used by the route report because lower Catfish Creek is controlled by Mississippi backwater. It is not a same-creek gauge.

thresholdSupport.notes: Wisconsin River Trips gives a route-specific Mississippi at Clayton stage ladder: 11 to 12 ft is a low but good target, 12.1 to 13 ft is average/decent, 14.1 to 16 ft is high, and 20.1+ ft may be too high for enjoyable paddling. The guidance is detailed, but the proxy-gauge nature keeps this below the app bar.

candidates.route: Akers Ferry to Pulltite
candidates.blocker: The route shape, direct Akers USGS gauge, and NPS public endpoint names are real, but Pulltite is not presently clean enough to ship as a dependable take-out. NPS currently says Pulltite Campground is closed for an unknown period after November 2024 flood damage, and the public threshold story is still only an average/closure marker rather than a route-specific runnable band.
candidates.retryCondition: Retry only after NPS clearly reopens Pulltite river-access operations and a stronger route-usable low-water floor emerges for the Akers gauge from NPS, USGS-adjacent manager guidance, or a comparably credible route-specific source.

gauge.notes: USGS 05389000 Yellow River near Ion, IA is a direct live same-river gauge in the lower Yellow River corridor. Wisconsin River Trips uses this gauge for the Yellow River ladder, and Miles Paddled reports the exact lower route against the Ion gauge.

thresholdSupport.notes: Wisconsin River Trips publishes the Ion-gauge Yellow River ladder: below 50 cfs likely too low, 51-100 cfs very shallow, 101-200 cfs shallow, 201-300 cfs average/good target, 301-600 cfs above average/high, 601-1000 cfs very high and expert-oriented, and 1001+ cfs maybe too high. Miles Paddled separately says the lower route needs enough water, identifies 80 cfs as a minimum caution, and ran it at 130 cfs.

endpoints.coordinateSource: Put-in coordinates reuse the existing Bridge Sixteen Canoe Access public-access coordinate already supported in app research, while Miles Paddled publishes matching Old Sixteen Road route GPS context and WRT directs paddlers to the public launch downstream/river left of Sixteen Bridge. Take-out coordinates come from Miles Paddled route GPS context for the Highway 76 / railroad bridge mouth-area finish, corroborated by NPS South Unit / Yellow River Boardwalk access context and Iowa DNR lower Yellow River State Forest canoe-access mapping.

candidates.notes: Implemented 2026-05-26 as a focused Iowa follow-on after the prompted candidates were already present.
